entry.title: The backbone of your HR success with Cloud Applications: Why Org Structure Design Matters
entry.content:
At SAP SuccessFactors, we’ve seen time and time again that a well-designed org structure in Employee Central can unlock significant business value, ensure consistent data, and support seamless integration across your entire HCM suite. Here’s a comprehensive overview of why org structure design is crucial and how to approach it effectively.

Organizational Structure Overview

At its core, an organizational structure defines the hierarchy and reporting relationships within a company. In the context of cloud foundations, this structure includes various levels such as business units, divisions, and departments.

These standard objects form the hierarchy through which people, processes, and systems interact. While seemingly straightforward, these elements are foundational for reporting, permissions, workflows, talent processes, and more.

Importantly, organizations also manage position structures—the "seats" within the org chart that employees move in and out of. These form the backbone of the position-to-position hierarchy, which many organizations are adopting as the leading model over traditional employee-to-employee hierarchies.

Key Factors Influencing Your Org Structure Design

Several factors influence the design of your organizational structure for cloud foundations:

  1. Business Strategy and Goals: Your structure should reflect how the organization plans to grow, restructure, or evolve.
  2. System of record: Whether you operate in a hybrid landscape or are fully cloud-based, Employee Central often becomes the authoritative source of truth for all downstream systems.
  3. Integration requirements: How your data flows to SAP S/4HANA, third-party systems, or talent modules matters greatly
  4. Regulatory and reporting needs: Local compliance, security access, and headcount visibility must be considered.

Org Structure Design Considerations

There are two primary approaches to designing your org structure in Employee Central:

  1. Standard 3-Level Hierarchy
    Use Business Unit > Division > Department—this is the most widely used approach (90–95% of customers). It's simple, supported by all standard reports and integrations, and ensures compatibility with the broader SuccessFactors suite.
  2. Extended Hierarchy with Customization
    If your organization requires more than three levels, consider reusing standard objects like Department in a nested structure or creating custom MDF objects. This approach allows for flexibility without sacrificing downstream compatibility—just be cautious of added complexity in reporting and user navigation.

Positions as Leading Hierarchy

In modern org design, positions are king. Moving away from traditional “chief” roles toward a position-to-position hierarchy offers:

  • Greater stability (less disruption from people changes)
  • Improved workflow flexibility (approval chains based on position IDs)
  • Future-proofing for innovations and enhancements
  • More robust succession, career, and talent planning

Whether or not you're using shared/mass positions, aiming for a 1:1 employee-to-position relationship is a best practice for reporting accuracy and streamlined administration.

Best Practices and Configuration Settings

To optimize your organizational structure for cloud foundations, consider these best practices:

  • Use standard foundation objects whenever possible
    This ensures better integration, maintenance, and support from SAP.
  • Design for scalability, not just today’s structure
    Consider future changes, potential reorganizations, and growth
  • Align permissions and workflows to your org hierarchy
    Dynamic roles and role-based permissions (RBP) should be structured around business units, divisions, or departments.
  • Establish position as your single point of job data
    Maintain job fields on the position object, sync one-way to job info.
  • Plan for change
    Communicate with stakeholders, plan your transitions, and test thoroughly—org structure changes are highly visible and impactful.

A strong org structure isn’t just about clean data—it’s about enabling agility, scalability, and trust across your business. Whether you are cloud-first or hybrid, designing your structure thoughtfully in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central ensures you are ready for whatever comes next.

Need support with your org design journey? Reach out to your Customer Success Manager or Account Executive to explore resources, planning tools, and upcoming webinars.

The original webinar & slides are available on-demand here:

Org Structure Design Principles & Best Practices - Apr 8

entry.title: Mass Data Management Support For Job Info
entry.content:
Introduction

The Mass Data Management tool allows Managers and HR Managers to efficiently update data for multiple team members at once. Until now, this feature was only available for Mass Changes of Position. Starting with the 2505 H1 release, it will also support updates to Job Information, expanding its functionality and making bulk data changes even more comprehensive.

MassChange Configuration For JobInfo

To configure mass changes for Position or Job Information, the prerequisite is enabling the corresponding permission.

Now Navigate to Manage Data--> MassChanges Job Configuration and create new configuration

 

Mass Change Configuration for Job Information can be created separately for each Person Type. Currently, we support two person types: Employee and Contingent Worker. The Person Type field is available exclusively for Job Information configurations, as the JobInfo data model is also defined for person type.

Once the Target Type and Person Type are selected, a list of fields specific to the chosen person type which are marked as visible in "Manage Business Configuration" are displayed—including country-specific fields. These fields can be configured as editable, filterable, or both, depending on the business need. A maximum of 15 fields can be configured, out of which up to 8 can be designated as default filters.

 

Field Configuration Restrictions

    • Company and Effective Start Date are system-defined default filters. These fields are automatically added by the system and cannot be configured as filterable or editable. 
    • The Position field cannot be set as editable. 
    • User-related fields such as Supervisor, Manager, etc., are not available for editable configuration. 
    • Fields containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII)  cannot be configured as either editable or filterable. 
    • The Attachment field is also excluded from being marked as editable or filterable. 
    • In case of cascading picklists and associated fields based on field criteria, only child field cannot be marked as editable. Both parent and child fields need to be marked as editable. 

Apply Action Execution Mode

Additinally for 2505 H1 release we do not support  configuration of  "Min Record Limit To Trigger Job" for Mass Change of Job Inforamtion. This will only be supported for Mass Change of Position.

This field controls how the Apply action in Mass Change is executed—either by triggering a background job to run asynchronously if the number of records exceed this configuration or by running the process synchronously in real time. 

In the current release for Job Information specific configuration, the system sets this field to a default value of 50, and only synchronous execution is supported. This means managers can apply changes to up to 50 Job Information records at a time through the Apply action. 

Mass Data Management for JobInfo

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

  • We should have the permission Manage Mass Data Management ->Enable Mass Data Management. 
  • We have the OData Administrator permission for edit or  User Permissions -> Edit Permission for Employee Central Effective Dated Entities  Job Information. 

If both the above mentioned permissions are available, on navigating to Mass Data Management screen and select create, we should see the below screen.

On selection of Job Information, we should see the drop down for person type , legal Entity and effective start date.

Once the Create button is selected on the screen above, the system loads the Mass Change Job Configuration based on the selected Job Information and Person Type. The user is then navigated to a new screen, which is automatically populated with the available filters—including the system-defined default filters: Start Date and Company.

When the Go button is selected, the screen loads Job Information records for various users based on the applied filters—such as Effective Date, Legal Entity, Location, Time Recording Profile, and Supervisor in this example. The results are further refined based on the target population and role-based permission checks .

As shown here, the Job Information records are listed, with the user display name in bold. By selecting the user name, we can navigate directly to the user's profile.

We can select up to 50 Job Information records and click the Edit button. This action will open a pop-up window showcasing the fields that have been configured as editable in the Mass Change Job Configuration.

As shown, the Event and Event Reason fields are mandatory. This is applicable when Event Derivation feature from rules is disabled.  We can view all the available events and event reasons, which are also listed in the Manage Self Service UI. Once the required fields are selected for editing, the Apply button will be enabled. Clicking the Apply button will trigger the On Change rules configured for the Mass Change rule context. Additionally, the Apply action will trigger certain backend processors needed for the Job Information records. These processes may include actions like nullifying the FTE value, which will be recalculated upon saving, or clearing child picklist values if the parent picklist value has changed. After the execution of Apply action, we can see the field changed by client and server under Modified fields section. 

Any server changed field configured as PII and RAL will not be listed under Modified fields section.

We also have an option discard the modification by selecting "Discard Modification Button" on some users. If not needed, we an just select Submit either immeditately or later.

The Mass Change job will be in the draft status after the execution of Apply action.

We can choose to submit the job whenever necessary. Upon clicking the Submit action Mass change job will be triggered which will execute all backend processors, on-save rules, and post-processors asycnchronously. During the excution of submit action, the Mass Change job will be in Processing  status.On completion of job, depending on the status of the records the status of the job changes to  Completed Successfully or Completed with Errors. 

Additional Details Regarding Job Status:

  • Workflow Support: We support workflows for Mass Change of Job Information. If a workflow is triggered, the job status will be marked as Completed Successfully. 
  • Log Details: The log will display the details of the error message, warning messages, triggered workflow. 
  • Error Handling: If any post-save processor fails on one of the records, all records will be marked as having an error. For example, a failure in time-off post-save processing could cause the entire transaction to fail. However, if a failure occurs in any of the processors before the save, only the affected record will be excluded from saving and marks as Error, while the other records will be saved successfully, and their status will be marked as Success. The job status for partial success will be marked as Completed with Errors, followed by the count of records that failed. 
  • If none of the records failed, then the job status would be Completed Successfully. 
  • The logs can be downloaded by clickling the Downlod Log button

The log is informative which will display errors and warnings for each of the records which was saved by mass changes

Conclusion

In conclusion, the Mass Data Management tool allows us to efficiently update Job Information for multiple users at once. It will always insert the updated Job Information records on the selected Effective Start Date, incorporating all the changes applied.

entry.title: Different ways to initiate onboarding for an internal hire.
entry.content:
When you discuss the onboarding process one of the most complex topics is how to handle internal hire onboarding or crossboarding as it is commonly called.  In this article we will look at the different options available.  We will focus more on the high-level processes rather than the detailed configuration required to achieve each step. 

To discuss this topic, we are going to set the following SuccessFactors landscape.  We are assuming the Employee Central is being used, with position management and event reason derivation. SuccessFactors recruitment and onboarding is being used and operates in three countries:

  • Germany has two legal entities.
  • United Kingdom has three legal entities
  • United States have one legal entity

As internal hire process can be complicated it is imperative that before you discuss it you need to have agreed your ‘New Hire Process’.  This is very useful as it allows you to form the baseline process for all internal hire conversation. Let’s assume the New Hire process is as follows

  1. Personal Data Collection including custom MDFs in a hire template
  2. Document signature
  3. Compliance (if applicable)
  4. MPH

There are some tasks, but these are not mandatory

We will talk about lots of different ways you can trigger internal hire onboarding processes, some may not be workable in some organizations but this article will hopefully help customers and partners to discuss the options available and make the best informed decision.

Understanding ‘how’ internal hires can move within the organization.

Before you start to discuss the internal hiring processes you need to confirm with payroll what is the impact of an internal move  on payroll.  In our example it has confirmed that in Germany you can switch between the legal entities without the need to have a termination and a hire record.  What this means if a person can switch positions in different legal entities with a simple ‘transfer’ event reason.  However, in the UK the only way a person can move between two legal entities is to terminate them on one and hire them into another entity.  This is dictated by payroll based on how the legal entities have been set up in UK, (this is just in our example),  In USA there is only one legal entity so this makes things simpler.

We have also confirmed that organization offer one way country transfer and concurrent employment. They don’t offer global assignments.

With this information we now know ‘how’ a person can move within the organization.  Now we can consider how the process is ‘Initiated’

Understand the different ways an internal hire process can be initiated

From SuccessFactors Recruitment.

This is the classic leading practice hiring process.  A job is advertised, an employee applies, they are selected, have an approved offer and ready to be hired.

If an internal hire has ‘Onboarding Initiated’ from recruitment then this will insert a future date row directly on the employee’s job information record in Employee Central.

The event reason will be defined by the event reason derivation logic and should that event reason have an associated workflow, the action will be subject to a pending approval.  Note they do not go into MPH!

Once the workflow is approved then an internal hire onboarding process can be triggered based on the specific process variant.  This process variant can be same as the New Hire process or different.

With this approach the employee central record is updated and this can sometimes create a challenge for some customers, in that do they have all the correct data in recruitment to update the record accurately.  Given that the record is already updated, how much of the ‘New Hire Process’ is relevant for the internal hire.  Can we assume that the persons employee central data is already up to date?  Do they need to sign any forms?  Remember this process can’t be used in UK for people switching legal entities.  This just leave tasks, and how relevant are these?  If someone is moving from one position to another and the manager doesn’t change, do you need any onboarding tasks? Hence it is worth spending time to consider what is needed from a data and process perspective for an internal hire.  Remember you may choose not to initiate an onboarding process.

The main downside of this process is twofold, not everyone applies for a job and some customers want more control before employee central is updated.

From Manage Pending hires

Another option is to send the candidate directly from recruitment to MPH, there is a great article written by my two colleagues on how to do this here .  The result is they are sent from recruitment to MPH.  When you process them from MPH, you can see the full EC record, you can make additional changes subject to RBP if required, validate the data and when comfortable submit the record.  The system will trigger event reason derivation and the associated workflow.  Once the workflow is approved an onboarding process can be triggered just like we described above.  Again, all the questions above about what should be in the onboarding process still apply.

This process is often overlooked but should be considered for customers who may want move control over the data before an onboarding process is triggered.

‘Managed Move’ from Employee central

However, we all know that not all internal moves come from recruitment, people can be promoted, transferred without going through recruitment.

This is what we we call a ‘Managed move’, a person moves from one position to another.  This can be done directly in employee central via ‘Take Action’, based on the event reason derivation, this will trigger a workflow and once this approved it can trigger an internal hire onboarding process. 

Again you need to discuss what in needed in a specific onboarding process for such a move.

Manually starting onboarding

In our example employees in the UK can’t switch between legal entities with a transfer event reason they need to have a hire and termination record.  For those who don’t come from recruitment you do have the option of manually starting onboarding.  This will start an onboarding process and once the rehire check has been conducted (providing they have a future dated termination) the hire can use the employment switcher to complete their data.  This process can be expanded for country transfers, and is particular useful as it allows the employee to have their record tied together and complete whilst employed in their current role.

Concurrent employment.

You can now trigger an onboarding process for concurrent employment, whether sent from recruitment or manually added in Employee Central.  Once the Employee Central record have been saved and approved an onboarding process can we initiated.

Global Assignments

These can also be used to trigger an onboarding process.

In all of the examples above I have talked about event reason derivation and workflow.  The workflow is not required this is just part of the EC process.

Hopefully this article highlights the different ways you can trigger an internal onboarding process and based on this allow you to decide on the what steps are needed in the each specific process.

entry.title: Calculate yearly absences pro-ratio for Compensation Planning
entry.content:
If you enjoy my blogs, please let me know by clicking on the Kudo button just above! Your support inspires me to write more.

 

Introduction: The Challenge

Recently, my friend Alex, a compensation consultant, approached me with an interesting problem. He said, "Stéphanie, I have a client who needs to determine the percentage of yearly presence for compensation purposes, so I can pro-rate the compensation calculation based on their presence rate. Can you think of something?" To which my response was, "Challenge accepted!"

In this blog, I'll share the Proof of Concept (PoC) I developed for Alex. It calculates the total number of days of absences (from time-off) and the presence rate for each employee for a specific year. Once this data is made available (in a custom object), Compensation Planning can work its magic with it. Although, to my knowledge, this PoC was not implemented, I thought it was too cool not to share and let it sit on a shelf!!

 

Solution: Version Simple

The solution is straightforward. I'll assume that if you're reading this blog, you have a basic understanding of creating custom MDF objects, rules, and attaching them to the object. So, let's skip to the important and fun stuff!

What I'm proposing is creating a custom MDF object (without an effective date). We'll add two decimal fields to it: one to store the number of absences in days (this field is technically optional, but it helps to explain the concept when both fields are visible) and another to store the attendance rate for the previous year (in this case, 2024), as shown below:

 

Once the object is created, you can add it to your People Profile (or, in my case, the Full Profile — how modern of me in early 2025! 😄).

Next, it's essential to know which rule functions are available. It's always a good practice to review the "Implementing Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors" guide periodically, especially the Function A-Z section, or to check the Change History section for new features (yes, I really do that).

Let's try using a function that seems appropriate for this case and see the results. (Remember, this is a proof of concept—there might be a better function to use, but this one will do the trick for now!):

 

Count Number of Leave of Absence Days()This function calculates the number of days for a given period of time.

 

Let's build our rule for 2024. In the first field, we'll count the number of days of absences in 2024. In the second field, we'll calculate the "Presence Rate," which is defined as:

where the Absence Rate is calculated as:

 

And then let’s assign our rule On Save in our beautiful custom object (as shown in my first image). Time to test:

  1. Go to an Employee File check out our Absence Count object:
  2. Select Edit on the object
  3. And Save

Our Absence Count shows 0 days of absence and a presence rate of 1 (or 100% present). Great!

Now, let's test when adding an absence:

  1. Add an absence in our employee Time Administration Workbench
  2. Go back to our object and select Edit
  3. And Save

Our Absence Count was updated to reflect 31 days of absence (the entire month of December 2024), and the presence rate was adjusted accordingly. Good again! It seems to work well.

The last step is to make the object available for all employees who were present in 2024. This is quite simple: you just need to create an import file for our custom object with the employees' IDs. The "On Save" rules will be triggered during the import, updating all employees in the file with their absence count and presence rate. Here is a sample of a one-line import and the result:

 

Solution: Version Intermediary!

I presented the simple version of the solution to Alex, to which he replied: "But Stéphanie, can you exclude some absences from your count? I need more flexibility. Also, if an employee was hired during the year, can you count the period before they were hired as an absence?". To which my response was, "Ok then, challenge accepted…again!"

There are numerous rule functions available for handling absences in SuccessFactors, such as:

 

Get Absence Days()With this function, you can calculate how long an employee has been absent within one year for a specific time type.
Get Absence In Days For Period()This function calculates cumulated absence days for the given date period and a given time type.
Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Calendar Days For Time Types()This rule function calculates the total number of absence days in given duration for the time types provided.
Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Working Days For Leave Of Absence Time Types()This rule function calculates the number of absence days during a given duration for the given time types.
Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Working Days For Time Types()This function calculates the cumulated absence days for the given duration and given time types.
Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Working Days For Time Types Excluding Weekdays()With this rule function, you can exclude the weekdays of your choice when getting the sum total of absence days
Get Absence in Days For Period with Threshold()This rule function calculates the sum of absence days in a given period for the time types provided where individual Employee Time duration exceeds a given threshold value.
Get Absence In Days Or Hours For Period Based On Deduction Quantity For Time Types()This rule function calculates the total deduction quantity, in days or hours, for absences in a specified period and with the specified time types.
Get Absence In Hours For Period()This function calculates cumulated absence hours for the given date period and a given time type.
Get Absence In Hours For Period For Time Types()This function calculates the cumulated absence hours for the given duration and given time types.
Get Completed Months Of Time Types In Period()This rule function calculates the number of complete months for which the employee was absent in a given period for the time types provided.
Get Number of Absences for Period for Time Types()This function calculates the number of absences of a list of time types within a given period.

(my point being, you can do a lot with rules when you explore the available functions and experiment with them!)

The function to use to count days of absence for specific time type (absence) is Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Calendar Days For Time Types().

For employees hired in 2024, we'll count the days between January 1, 2024, and their hire date, adding this sum to our absence count.

I implemented these three calculations as variables:

Now, we'll set up two conditions:

  • For employees hired before 2024: We'll count their absences for specific time types and calculate the presence rate.
  • For employees hired during 2024: We'll add the hire pro-rata to our absence count.

Now, let’s test!

Paternity Leave (6003) is not included in our absence count. Let’s see the result of our object when an employee goes on Paternity Leave:

 

Long Term Disability (6001) is included in our absence count. Let’s see the result of our object when an employee goes on Long Term Disability:

Finally, for employees hired during the year (2024), the days prior to their hire are included in our absence count. Let's see the result of our object for an employee hired on July 1st, 2024:

There we go, challenge resolved! Now, we just need to load our import file again to update the values for all employees.

 

Solution: Version Expert!

I went back to Alex to present the intermediary version of the solution. Alex said, "This is great, Stéphanie! I could use this in compensation planning. But for 2025, can you make your object reflect live calculations as time passes?”. Truth be told, Alex did not push it there, but I thought it would have been a good follow-up question. I also wanted to implement this it because it would look better in my demo system 😊.

So, here it goes...

For the expert mode solution, we'll create two new fields for the year 2025 (the ongoing year at the time of writing this blog) on our custom objects.

  • We'll need to have IF conditions of the rule so that our absence count and our pro-rata are dynamic as time advances.
  • Additionally, we'll have IF conditions for when the year is finished and the counter become static.

Here is a look at our variables, what they do, and where we will use them in our rule:

 

And now, the rule itself! (It's starting to look a bit more complicated, but remember, we're in the Expert version of the solution!):

What's left is to trigger the rule automatically every day so that the counts and rates get updated as time passes in 2025!

To achieve this, we'll need to use a mix of Integrations in Integration Center and Intelligent Services. I won't go into the details of Integration Center and Intelligent Services here (this blog has already taken way longer to write than planned), but I like the setup with the following three integrations:

 

 

Absence Count – Intelligent Service - Hire

Trigger: Hire intelligent Service
Description: This integration will initialize / create the portlet for newly hired employees. Simply map the employee user ID to the external code of the custom portlet!
Note that if you are going-live with this solution you should to an upload of the custom object on all active employee prior to the go-live to initialize the portlet.

Absence Count – Intelligent Service – Time-Off

Trigger: Time-Off intelligent Service
Description: This integration will update the portlet whenever a time-off is saved in the system.

Absence Count – Schedule Daily Job

Trigger: Schedule daily job
Description: This integration will update the portlet daily, calculating days of absences and the presence rate as 2025 progresses.

Now, just ensure your intelligent services (hire and time-off) are set up to trigger the integrations mentioned above and schedule the third one. And there you go! The custom objects will reflect live data and get updated every day. Expert mode solution in a nutshell!

 

Conclusion

To finish…

First, the goal of this blog was to demonstrate how to make absence/presence pro-ratio data available in SuccessFactors for use in the Compensation Planning module. I presented three solutions to achieve this. I've developed these as proof of concepts and never had the chance to implement them. So, if you get to use this solution, please let me know. It will make me happy to know it was useful!

Second, many thanks to my friend, Alex Rodriguez, for bringing me these challenges every now and then!

Lastly, I just want to say that now I know how hard it is to keep it short when writing a blog about time 😉!

Cheers,

Stéphanie

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central : 1H 2025
entry.content:
In the series of 1H 2025 release blogs, my next talks about SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. The 1H 2025 release brings a fresh wave of enhancements to Employee Central, focused on simplifying HR processes, enhancing mobile usability, improving benefits handling, and strengthening document management.

From event-driven automation in Position Management to AI-powered support through Joule, and important upgrades in Benefits and Document Templates, this release continues SAP’s commitment to delivering a more intelligent, efficient, and people-centered HR experience.

Whether you're an administrator optimizing core HR, or an HR leader improving employee services, these updates are built to support your goals.

1. A Smoother Mobile People Profile Experience

Feature: Enhanced People Profile in Mobile
Why it matters: Employee data is now easier to browse on mobile.

Image Source: SAP

The People Profile in the mobile app has been improved for faster navigation and better usability. Accessing and managing employee data on the go is now simpler and more efficient.

Few Use Cases: With the basic integration between SAP SuccessFactors and Microsoft Teams enabled, users can now initiate a Teams chat directly from the Profile Preview. Additionally, an indicator displaying job title and employment status is now available for contingent workers — a feature that was previously limited to individuals with multiple employments.

2. More Insights, Fewer Clicks in Full Profile

Feature: Expanded Full Profile View
Why it matters: See the full picture of your workforce.

Additional cards are now available in the Full Profile, giving users more visibility into employee details from a single screen. This update supports deeper insight and quicker decision-making.

3. Event-Based Position Updates

Feature: Position Follow-Up After Imports
Why it matters: Automation reduces manual data maintenance.

An event-based solution now handles updates triggered by Job Information and Termination imports. This ensures cleaner data and reduces manual follow-up work in Position Management.

⚠️Note: The follow-up processes after importing Job Information with the Termination event are the same as after a Termination Details import.

4. Smarter Admin Alerts for Position Management

Feature: Admin Alerts for Import Issues
Why it matters: Stay proactive with position-related errors.

 Image Source: SAP

Admins can now track issues in Position Management imports via Admin Alerts. This helps catch and resolve errors early to maintain data accuracy.

5. Digital Signature Placeholders in Document Templates

Feature: Placeholder Support for Digital Signatures
Why it matters: Enable signing workflows directly in templates.

You can now embed digital signature placeholders in Document Templates. This makes it easier to trigger signing processes automatically — a big step forward for digital document management and paperless HR operations.

Permissions Needed:

  • View, Edit, and Import/Export permission under Manage Permission Roles  Miscellaneous Permissions  Configure Digital Signing for Document Generation.
  • View, Correct, Create, Adjust Order, Delete, and Import/Export permission under Manage Permission Roles  Miscellaneous Permissions  Configure Digital Signing for Document Generation.Signers (Signers).

⚠️Note: In the previous release 2H 2024, the feature was under restricted availability. In the 1H 2025 release, it is generally available.

6. Enabling Benefit Level Workflow in the Latest Enrollment Process

Feature: Benefit-Level Approval Workflow
Why it matters: More precise control over benefit changes.

The latest benefit enrollment process now supports workflows at the individual benefit level, allowing you to route changes for approval based on the specific benefit selected. This adds a new layer of flexibility and control in benefits management.

⚠️Note: The new field allows you to control the triggering of workflows for benefit enrollment requests more efficiently, during open enrollment. This field has two dropdown options:

  • Group Specific: Choosing this will allow you to trigger a single workflow for all the benefits. This is the current behaviour.
  • Benefit Specific: Choosing this will allow you to trigger workflows configured at the benefit level.

7. Recording the Waive Action by the Employee for Benefits

Feature: Track Benefit Waivers
Why it matters: Gain visibility into declined benefits.

Image Source: SAP 

When employees choose to waive a benefit during enrollment, that action is now recorded in the system. This improves compliance and gives HR a clearer understanding of employee benefit preferences.

8. Benefit Summary in the Group Enrollment Workflow Request

Feature: Summary View in Group Enrollment
Why it matters: Approvers see the full picture.

A benefit summary is now included in the Group Enrollment Workflow Request, making it easier for approvers to quickly review and validate employee elections before approval.

Images Source: SAP 

⚠️Note: This feature is only applicable for the latest benefits enrollment experience. Benefits summary details is not visible when the benefits enrollment happens through the legacy experience.

9. Generative AI Empowers HR on the Go

Feature: Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
Why it matters: AI assistance just got mobile.

SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, is now available in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app. Users can interact with HR systems using natural language to get quick answers, complete tasks, and navigate employee data — all from their smartphones.

Final Thoughts

The 1H 2025 release for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central delivers powerful improvements across mobile, workflows, position management, benefits, and documentation. From AI-driven efficiencies to compliance-ready benefits features, these updates continue to support a more flexible and intelligent HR landscape. Be sure to review these enhancements in your preview environment, configure where needed, and communicate key changes with your teams to maximize adoption.

For full technical documentation, visit the SAP SuccessFactors What's New Viewer or connect with your SAP support representative.

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central?
entry.content:
The First Half 2025 Release delivers many exciting innovations within SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central. We are introducing new features and capabilities designed to make finding information and completing tasks quick and easy. Here is a snapshot of what you can expect to see in this release:

People Profile Enhancements
Building upon a successful launch of our new People Profile, we are continuing to enhance the experience by bringing in additional content from across SAP SuccessFactors solutions, including benefits, payroll, and learning.

Additional Learning and Benefits Content in the People Profile


To further support the unique requirements of public sector organizations, we are also introducing a new portlet that displays the financing status of an employee directly within their profile.

Financing Status in the People Profile


Addressing Customer Influence Requests
We are pleased to address multiple customer influence requests with new capabilities designed to boost efficiency for all users. Managers can now create job requisitions directly from the My Team Positions view, reducing the need to navigate between pages. In addition, attachments can now be uploaded within quick actions to help ensure smooth task completion when further documentation is required.

Attachments in Quick Actions


To simplify the administrator experience, we are also introducing support for bulk changes to employee data within the mass data management tool, enabling non-technical users to quickly update job information, such as cost centers and holiday calendars.

Mass Changes to Job Information


These are just a few of the many innovations in the SAP SuccessFactors First Half 2025 Release. To learn more, check out the resources below:

  • Visit the SAP SuccessFactors Community for release documentation, product videos, and solution area blogs
  • Watch the 1H 2025 Release Highlights Overview Video, featuring Bianka Woelke, GVP Head of Application Product Management

entry.title: Restrict leave application based off remaining balance of another time type
entry.content:
Business Requirement: 

To only allow application of unpaid leave if there's no more annual leave balance left.

Prerequisite:

  • Time Profile with the related Time Types has been created
    • Annual Leave Time Type has already been created
    • Unpaid Leave Time Type has already been created

Resolution: 

This can be achieved by creating a validation rule that checks the remaining balance of annual leave and restricting application of unpaid leave.

Create message definition:

 

Here, we'll be creating the error message where system displays the remaining annual leave balance. Alerting users to use them instead.

 Create Validation Rule:

 

 

We create a variable to check & store the remaining balance of annual leave time type as of unpaid leave application date. If there's still balance left, error message displays. 

Assign this validation rule to Unpaid Leave Time Type

 

 

Testing:

  • Assign Time Profile to employee
  • When employee applies unpaid leave but still has remaining annual leave balance, system should restrict application

     

     

Employee still have 10 days Annual Leave Balance.

 

 

Employee cannot proceed to unpaid leave application as there's still Annual Leave balance left. Remaining balance is also displayed.

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: Innovations galore in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone! We are very excited to share with you all the innovations we are delivering in the first half 2025 release for our favorite “SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll” (ECP) solution. Some of these are already being called “game changing” innovations in the marketplace. You will be thrilled to see that SAP’s product and engineering teams have kept the promises it made during SuccessConnect roadmap and strategy sessions in Lisbon last October. Have a look at the for the full release notes and all of the details for everything we are delivering.  

 

AI-enabled “Explain my pay statement” capability via Joule

We are proud to introduce the “Explain My Pay statement” capability. Now, employees can easily understand details of their pay statements by simply asking Joule, SAP’s AI copilot. For example, requests like compare my historical pay statements, explain a specific deduction and other amounts on a payslip. This reduces HR inquiries and tickets, and boosts employee satisfaction. The replies by Joule will be based on the company’s policy documents. And by the way, Joule is now also available on mobile making it even more convenient to get your work done anywhere, at any time. 

Available in English and in US, UK and Australia locales at launch. Other languages and locales to follow our planned country roadmap.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”


Explain my pay statement feature: Prompt “Compare my payslip from last month to this month”


Explain my pay statement feature: Prompt “why is my deduction so high”

 

Enhanced Payroll Control Center (PCC) for admins

Check out our Enhanced Payroll Control Center through which business users can now manage a complete payroll cycle from start to end, not just running the payroll calculation, but all payroll activities that are necessary. We offer easy to configure payroll process flow and dashboards without IT skills which streamlines operations.

This significantly enhanced user experience provides real-time insights from pre-payroll activities -  such as gathering time and attendance records, collecting benefits and deductions information, to post-payroll activities such as bank transfer, statutory reporting, and internal reporting.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”


Complete payroll cycle view from start to end


Enhanced Payroll Control Center dashboard

 

Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for cloud Integration with Employee Central Payroll

We are thrilled to announce the integration of the Position Budgeting Control Workbench with ECP. Combined with the existing integrations of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and SAP S/4 HANA cloud, this innovation provides live insights into personnel budget utilization and costs. This enables better management of allocated funds and grants, giving full visibility of salary costs, committed budget and available funds for personnel expenses especially in regulated industries such as Public Sector, Education and Research, healthcare and more.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

Access to payroll information for terminated employees (alumni)

Our offboarding solution now allows terminated employees and company alumni to access payroll information (for example, pay statements or tax documents for a specified time after the termination date). This avoids manual effort for payroll administrators to provide documents to terminated employees.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

 

New Local Version – Bulgaria

With this release we are excited to now provide the ability to run SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll for Bulgaria. This now brings our localized versions to 53 native locales, highest in the industry. (See the full list on our localization site with a cool interactive map). Native local country versions support our global base of customers with a local, compliant, and proven payroll platform. We also have five additional partner countries available as described in SAP KBA 3031216 - Employee Central Payroll: Availability of Partner Local Versions. EPI-USE has localized Peru, Vietnam, Denmark, Namibia, and Angola.

Our strategy is to deliver local versions we offered on-premise based upon customer and prospect demand so more to come for localizations. We also have a high interest from various partners to continue to localize more versions.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2025 release, including

  • Joule Available in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
  • Enhancements to the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
  • Replication of Dependents for Malaysia
  • Report for Checking and Creating Jobs, Positions, and Organizational Units Available for Chile
  • And more

See the “What's New Viewer” for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2023 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2023 Release Highlight Blog 

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

entry.title: How to migrate Boomi Integrations to SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
1. Purpose of the document

This blog provides a comprehensive guide on migrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM integrations from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS), highlighting its strategic advantages, including prepackaged integrations, Tech Envo Accelerators, Migration Framework, Migration Process,  Design considerations, and hybrid deployment options. It covers SAP’s runtime environments, deployment models, and TechEnvo’s migration accelerators, ensuring a seamless migration. With TechEnvo’s expertise and adherence to SAP best practices, organizations can modernize their integration landscape while ensuring scalability, consistency, and long-term success.

 

2. Why SAP SuccessFactors HCM customer needs to migrate from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite

  • Between 2013 and 2016, SAP SuccessFactors HCM customers used BOOMI as an integration platform to support data integration requirements in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation, and many customers are still running on Boomi.
  • SAP Customers have significantly invested in SAP BTP and SAP Integration Suite in the last seven years as part of S4HANA transitions and Integration Modernization (moving from SAP PO to SAP IS). 
  • Customers are migrating interfaces from Boomi to the SAP Integration Suite, aligning with the SAP Integration strategy.
  • Maintaining multiple integration platforms adds a lot of Technical Debt, and SAP customers consolidate and move to one integration platform (SAP Integration Suite) 
  • SAP Integration Suite Capabilities on par with BOOMI

3. SAP Integration Suite Capabilities and Features

SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS) is the strategic integration platform for SAP customers, offering seamless connectivity with over 3,400 prebuilt integrations and 3,800+ APIs. It provides all the capabilities of Boomi and additional advantages such as prepackaged content covering most of the SAP Solutions Integrations, AI-driven integration, and enhanced security. 

SAP Integration Suite is a comprehensive set of tools and services that enable seamless integration across various SAP and third-party systems. It helps businesses connect, automate, and optimize their processes across cloud and on-premises environments. Here are some key features and components of the SAP Integration Suite:

3.1 Key Features:

  • Connectivity: Provides pre-built connectors and adapters for integrating various SAP and third-party applications, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and external applications like Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics.
  • Integration Flows: Facilitates the orchestration of data between applications with customizable integration flows, enabling businesses to automate workflows and synchronize data.
  • API Management: Allows businesses to manage their APIs in a secure and scalable manner, with tools to create, expose, and govern APIs across multiple environments.
  • Data Transformation: Supports the transformation of data between different formats (e.g., XML, JSON) to ensure seamless communication between systems.
  • Monitoring and Analytics: Provides dashboards and monitoring tools for real-time visibility into integration processes and system health, allowing proactive management.
  • Cloud-Based: Being cloud-native, SAP Integration Suite provides flexibility and scalability to handle evolving integration needs.
  • Security: Offers robust security features, including authentication, authorization, encryption, and secure communication for data exchanges

3.2 SAP Integration Suite Core Components:

  1. SAP Cloud Integration (CPI): A cloud-based integration platform for connecting SAP and non-SAP applications. It allows seamless integration of cloud and on-premise systems.
  2. SAP API Management: A solution to design, publish, and monitor APIs, providing tools to securely expose and consume APIs.
  3. SAP Open Connectors: A collection of pre-built connectors to a variety of third-party applications and services.
  4. SAP Integration Advisor: A tool that automates the mapping and transformation of data across systems, helping streamline the integration process.
  5. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): While the Integration Suite is a part of BTP, it works closely with other BTP services like SAP HANA, SAP AI, and SAP analytics for a comprehensive digital transformation solution.

3.3 SAP Integration Suite Use Cases:

  • ERP Integration: Connect SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SAP S/4HANA with other enterprise systems, such as CRM, Payroll, and Time, Benefits..etc, to ensure a unified view of business operations.
  • Cloud and On-Premises Integration: Link cloud-based applications (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors HCM ) with on-premises systems (e.g., SAP ECC).
  • API-First Strategy: Enable businesses to expose services as APIs and integrate them with external systems for broader connectivity.
  • Data Synchronization: Automatically sync data between systems, ensuring consistency and eliminating manual data entry errors.

3.4 SAP Integration Suite Benefits:

  • Faster Time-to-Value: Pre-built integrations and connectors reduce implementation time.
  • Scalability: Easily scale integration scenarios as your business grows and systems evolve.
  • Flexibility: The suite supports various integration scenarios, from simple point-to-point integrations to complex, multi-cloud architectures.
  • Cost Efficiency: Reduces integration complexity and costs by leveraging a unified platform for all integration needs.

In summary, SAP Integration Suite enables organizations to drive digital transformation by ensuring seamless integration across their entire IT landscape, whether using SAP or third-party applications.

 

4.Boomi to SAP Integration Suite Migration – Tech Envo Framework 

We have worked on multiple Boomi to SAP Integration Suite migration projects and developed a framework and strategy for successful migrations.

 

4.1 Scope of Boomi Integrations to be migrated to SAP Integration Suite:

This is the first step in the migration process: identifying the number of integrations currently running in the Boomi Production Atom environment and ensuring the scope is determined. 

4.2 Document pain points and current environment: 

Talk to the business and technical team supporting the integrations in the BOOMI environment and get a list of pain points and challenges. This is a critical step, and addressing these issues in the SAP Integration Suite after migration is necessary.

Common pain points : 

  1. Poor Integration design 
  2. Poor Integration coding standards 
  3. Not implementing prepackaged integrations
  4. Customizing prepackaged integrations 
  5. Heavy Groovy scripting
  6. Lack of better error handling mechanisms
  7. Lack of documentation
  8. Not using the correct SAP SuccessFactors APIs
  9. Delta Logic is missing 
  10. No encryption
  11. Authentication type issues 
  12. Tightly coupled interfaces - not easy to make updates.

4.3 Identify Integration Patterns: 

Identity integration patterns like Synchronous, Asynchronous, cloud-to-cloud, cloud-to-on-premises, On-premise—on-premise Integration, Scheduled, Real-Time, Type of Systems involved, etc., and create an SAP Integration Suite Integration patterns covering all scenarios. 

  • Synchronous Integration – Real-time data exchange with immediate response—API-to-API communication between SAP SuccessFactors and another system.


                   Fig: Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution flow of Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync

  • Asynchronous Integration – Data is sent without waiting for an immediate response.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync.

Fig:  High Resolution flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync Integration.

  • Cloud-to-Cloud Integration – Data exchange between two cloud applications.Syncing data between SAP SuccessFactors and another cloud-based system(ServiceNow,Okta, Workday,SAP Concur, SAP Fieldglass, S4HANA Cloud.etc)

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution Flowof SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

  • Cloud-to-On-Premises Integration – Communication between a cloud-based system and an on-premises system.Triggering updates in an on-premises system from a cloud-based application(S4 HANA OP, SAP ECC, Data Lake OP, SFTP OP).

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

  • On-Premises to On-Premises Integration – Data exchange between two on-premises systems.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig:  Integration Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

  • Scheduled Integration – Batch processing executed at defined intervals.Daily batch transfer of employee records from SAP SuccessFactors.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

Fig: High Resolution flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

  • Real-Time Integration – Event-driven or API-based real-time integration.Triggering real-time updates in one system based on events in another.
  • Employee Demographic Data Outbound Integrations

Fig: Active directory(Outbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

  • Employee Data Update Inbound Integrations

Fig: Integration Flow of Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

4.4 Create Migration Strategy 

  • Create a detailed strategy covering all aspects of migration:
  1. Deployment Approach and Timelines
  2. Prioritization of interfaces
  3. Technical Architecture
  4. Documenting best practices 

4.5 Map Boomi capabilities with SAP Integration Suite

Identify capabilities used in BOOMI and map which capabilities you need to use in IS. This will give a clear picture of gaps in capabilities and how they can be solved, such as connectors used, API management, Scripting, security, Retry mechanism, error logging capabilities, etc. 

Tech Envo's best practices always include using standard capabilities offered by the SAP integration suite and implementation prepacked integration before building custom integrations.  

4.6 Create Technical Architecture / Design and Development standards

Creating a technical design for each interface is critical. The technical design can be reused depending on the integration type and pattern. Create detailed technical design standards and a framework for the topics below. 

  • Development standards
  • Naming conventions
  • Reusable framework / a common framework (reuse of scripts, security materials, etc)
  • Documentation standards and deliverables
  • Interface development life cycle
  • Interface testing 
  • API best practices 

4.7 Integration Build and Testing

Ensure that the integration consultant working on the integration build has the expertise below. This will help build high-quality integrations. 

  • A clear understanding of SAP SuccessFactors HCM APIs 
  • Excellent hands-on experience in the SAP Integration suite 
  • Experience in Integration build for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • Able to understand BOOMI integration code (Boomi platform)

Create a detailed development process for all integrations, align on the development approach, and implement leading practices. This will ensure consultants working on the project follow the same standards and help support the interfaces better.

For example, if you are building delta logic for SAP SuccessFactors HCM data extract, ensure this logic can be reusable across interfaces. Usually, we create a reusable Groovy script. Maintaining the code would be less effort if everyone used the same approach.

4.8 Integrations Testing and comparing BOOMI/SAP Integration Suite Outputs. 

Integration testing is a critical phase of the project. Ensure you document all test cases, check if any are available from the Boomi project, and enhance the same with additional cases. Run the integration in the Boomi environment and SAP Integration suite and compare data. If data matches both platforms, logic integration in SAP integration suits and covers all requirements but follows this process for all scenarios. 

4.9 Deployment in SAP Integration Suite Production 

Once the business approves the integration, plan the deployment in the SAP Integration Suite production environment and schedule as required. Un-deploy or stop the Boomi schedule for the interfaces deployed in the SAP IS prod environment. 

5. Boomi to SAP Cloud Integration - Migration High-Level Decision Flow to determine Integration approach 

The primary goal of the below decision is to leverage prepackaged integrations as much as possible before building a custom integration solution. This approach aligns with the recommendation of SAP's leading practices and SAP clean core principles.

 

 

6. Tech Envo Migration Accelerators

TechEnvo Migration Accelerators include 74+ prepackaged integrations and templates for SAP SuccessFactors HXM in SAP Integration Suite, reusable Framework, and an advanced error-handling framework. Our team is experts in Boomi, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions, with deep knowledge of SAP SuccessFactors APIs and best practices. With 700+ integrations built using SAP Integration Suite and Boomi, we bring extensive migration experience, ensuring a seamless transition from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite. Our certified professionals have successfully executed multiple Boomi-to-SAP IS migration projects, leveraging industry-leading methodologies.
